title: HMW gDNA purification proto
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3prco","text":"Optimized protocol for efficient extraction of HMW gDNA from the polysaccharide-rich microalga Botryococcus, enabling long-read sequencing on the Oxford Nanopore Technologies platform. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":95,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"97t0","text":"Maintain Frozen Samples: It is crucial to keep samples frozen during maceration using liquid nitrogen. Prepare sterile mortar, pestle, and spatulas for this step. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"90beo","text":"Handle Homogenization with Care: Exercise caution with the use of liquid nitrogen during maceration and sampling preparation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"19mjb","text":"Optimize Polysaccharide Removal: For effective polysaccharide removal with minimal DNA damage, keep the sonication step prior to cell lysis brief and at a low power setting. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2b9p5","text":"Monitor Pellet Size: After each sorbitol wash step, check for an increase in pellet size. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6sk2q","text":"Buffer Preparation: Prepare and sterilize buffers in advance to streamline the process. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7gjls","text":"Warm DNA Extraction Buffer: Warm the DNA extraction buffer to 65°C for at least 5 minutes before use for optimal results. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"jdg3","text":"Ensure Bench Cleanliness: Prior to starting the protocol, thoroughly sanitize lab bench and instruments. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5ld3p","text":"A. Buffer preparation ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6g2e","text":"1. Sorbitol wash buffer. Autoclave and store this buffer at 4°C for no more than six months. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"807l2","text":"• 100 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3je9u","text":"• 0.35 M Sorbitol ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eli7v","text":"• 5 mM EDTA pH 8.0 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2r5tk","text":"• 1 % (W/V) Polyvinylpyrrolidone molecular weight 40,000 (PVP-40) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bnh89","text":"• 1% (V/V) 2-Mercaptoethanol (β-ME). Note: Add after autoclaving and before use. It is best to aliquot the amount of buffer needed and add β-ME to this aliquot. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6d4n1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pna0","text":"2. DNA extraction buffer. Autoclave and store this buffer at room temperature for no more than six months. Note: Add after autoclaving and before use. It is best to aliquot the amount of buffer needed and add β-ME to this aliquot. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8k84o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cglda","text":"4. 3M Sodium acetate buffer ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9nq3b","text":"• 408.3 g sodium acetate ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"j81k","text":"• 3H2O per L ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"90ref","text":"• pH to 5.2 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vghi","text":"• autoclave ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2butd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4o71c","text":"5. 1x TE (Tris EDTA) Buffer ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"574ek","text":"• 1mM EDTA, pH 8.0 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"32kip","text":"• 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vini","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5e91q","text":"B. Culturing Botryococcus culturing ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36},{"style":"italic","offset":13,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6i9fv","text":"• Culture Botryococcus species of choice in 1 L roux flasks with 750 ml modified Chu 13 medium, pH 7.5. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":10,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6lp3n","text":"• Maintain at 22°C under continuous aeration with 2.5% CO2. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"70lat","text":"• Grow cultures for 6 weeks under a 12 h light:12 h dark cycle using 13 W compact fluorescent 65 K lighting at an intensity of 280 μmol photons/m2/s. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Biomass preparation • Harvest the biomass by filtration using a 10 μm nylon net.

• Add 1 ml sorbitol wash buffer to 1.5 ml eppendorf tube containing ~100 mg ground biomass. • Allow sample to thaw while vortexing for 10 seconds • Keep samples on ice and sonicate for 25 seconds at 30% of power. • Centrifuge at 2,500 x g for 5 minutes at room temperature. Discard the liquid phase by aspiration or decanting. Save the pelleted and floating biomass. • Repeat the biomass pre-wash step three times.

• Add 700 μl DNA extraction buffer pre-warmed to 65°C, homogenize by vortexing for 10 seconds. • Incubate at 65°C for 30 minutes mixing by inversion every 10 minutes. • Incubate samples at room temperature for 5 minutes. • Add 700 μl CHCl3:IAA buffer, vortex for 10 seconds, and centrifuge at 2,500 x g for 10 minutes at room temperature. • Carefully transfer the upper aqueous phase (approximately 500 μl) to a new 1.5 ml eppendorf tube and keep on ice.

• Place small amount of frozen biomass into mortar and pestle with liquid nitrogen. • Grind biomass until a fine powder is formed, keeping frozen at all times. • Weigh out ~100 mg aliquots, pace in 1.5 ml eppendorf tube, and store at -80℃.

• Collect small amounts of biomass from the mesh using a rubber spatula and immediately freeze by placing in a 50 ml Falcon tube containing liquid nitrogen. • Repeat until all biomass is collected into a single Falcom tube. • Store at -80℃ until needed.

• Add 2 μl RNase A (25 mg/ml), and incubate at 37°C for 15 minutes mixing by inversion every 5 minutes. • Add 500 μl CHCl3:IAA buffer, vortex 5 seconds, and centrifuge at 13,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C. • Transfer the upper phase to a new 1.5 ml eppendorf tube and keep on ice.

• Precipitate the HMW gDNA by adding 0.1 volumes of 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and 0.66 volumes of cold (-20°C) isopropanol. • Incubate samples overnight at -20°C. • Centrifuge at 13,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C, and discard supernatant by aspiration or decanting. • Dry pellet by resting inverted on paper towels at room temperature.

• Wash dried pellets with 1 ml of 70% ethanol and invert several times. • Centrifuge at 13,000 x g for 10 min at 4°C. Remove the supernatant by aspiration to avoid pellet disturbance. • Dry samples in a vacuum centrifuge for 10 min at 36°C. • Resuspend HMW gDNA by adding 100 μl 1x TE buffer. Incubate at room temperature for 10 min then gently homogenize by inversion. • Avoid pipetting that will shear the DNA. • Store at -80°C until needed.
creator: Ivette Cornejo Corona
